question: Explain the management of radiation exposure for RPV materials.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the methodology used to calculate these limits, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides the resulting pressure-temperature curves and values to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures to ensure the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI codes and relevant regulatory conditions for ongoing safety and integrity verification.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV) is a key component that houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer, serving as a primary barrier to fission product release and supporting various reactor systems. The RPV and its appurtenances are constructed from materials specified in the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR 50, including General Design Criteria (GDC) for quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture prevention. The materials used for Class 1 components, such as the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and steam generators (SGs), are specified in Table 5.2-4 and are subject to strict quality assurance and fracture toughness criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and supports various reactor components, with its materials and fabrication adhering to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as relevant ASME code cases related to materials reliability, water chemistry, and quality assurance for nuclear facility applications.

edges.description: Section A describes specific weld examination and compliance requirements for RPV materials, while Section B outlines broader inspection, shipment, and storage requirements that reference compliance with ASME codes, thus Section A provides detailed specifications for the general inspection framework described in Section B.

Section A describes specific examination and welding requirements for RPV attachments per ASME BPVC, while Section B outlines the material selection and inspection/testing requirements for RPV threaded fasteners, both referencing ASME BPVC standards to ensure the integrity and safe management of radiation exposure for RPV materials.

Section A specifies examination and welding requirements for RPV attachments in accordance with ASME BPVC, providing detailed implementation for the general description of RPV materials and components outlined in Section B.

Section A describes specific examination and welding requirements for RPV attachments in accordance with ASME BPVC, while Section B establishes that all RCPB materials, including those in the RPV, must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus Section A provides detailed implementation for the general compliance framework set in Section B.

Section A specifies inspection and welding requirements for RPV attachments in accordance with ASME BPVC, providing detailed implementation for the general RPV description and function outlined in Section B.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for RPV attachments and welds, while Section B lists references including EPRI chemistry guidelines and ASME quality assurance standards that underpin or inform the regulatory basis for those requirements.

Section A specifies the handling, storage, and inspection requirements for RPV materials to prevent radiation-induced degradation, while Section B details the selection and qualification of threaded fastener materials for the RPV to ensure integrity under radiation exposure, both referencing ASME BPVC standards as a connecting regulatory basis.

Section A outlines the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the RPV, which are necessary to ensure the integrity and proper management of radiation exposure for the RPV materials described in Section B.

Section A describes the handling, storage, and inspection requirements for the RPV that reference ASME BPVC standards, while Section B specifies that the RPV materials must conform to those same ASME BPVC standards, thus Section A provides implementation details for the general material requirements outlined in Section B.

Section A specifies the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the RPV, which are applied to the RPV described in Section B, thereby ensuring the management of radiation exposure for the RPV materials.

Section A describes the handling, storage, and environmental controls for the RPV in accordance with ASME NQA-1-2008/1a-2009 Addenda, which is also referenced in Section B as a quality assurance requirement for nuclear facility applications, connecting them through shared regulatory standards for material management.

Section A specifies the material selection and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners in the RPV, which are a subset of the overall RPV materials described in Section B, thereby providing detailed implementation for the general RPV material context relevant to radiation exposure management.

Section A specifies the use and selection criteria of threaded fastener materials for the RPV, providing detailed implementation of the general material conformance requirements outlined in Section B.

Section A describes the selection and inspection of threaded fastener materials for the RPV to ensure integrity under radiation exposure, while Section B provides the overall structural context and function of the RPV within the reactor coolant system, connecting the specific material management in A to the broader vessel described in B.

Section A describes the selection and qualification of RPV threaded fastener materials in accordance with industry standards, while Section B lists the specific references (such as EPRI chemistry guidelines and ASME QA requirements) that underpin and justify the material choices and practices described in Section A.

Section A describes the RPV as a component of the reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies that the materials used for the RPV (as part of the RCPB) must conform to ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, directly connecting the RPV's material management to compliance standards.

Section A describes the reactor vessel materials and their role in the reactor system, while Section B lists references, including guidelines and standards, that provide requirements and best practices for maintaining the integrity and managing the radiation exposure of these materials.
Section A specifies the regulatory and material standards that the RPV (described in Section B) must meet to ensure proper management of radiation exposure for RPV materials.

Section A describes the regulatory and material standards for RPV materials, while Section B lists references (such as EPRI chemistry guidelines and ASME QA requirements) that provide supporting technical bases and guidance for the management of radiation exposure and material integrity discussed in Section A.

Section A describes the physical configuration and function of the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), while Section B lists references, including water chemistry and materials reliability guidelines, that inform the management of radiation exposure and material integrity for the RPV.
